message:
feat(search): add hypothesis similarity search (c2u8)

Implement hypothesis similarity search for finding related hypotheses
across sessions using local hash-based embeddings.

Features:
- hypothesisToSearchText: Convert hypothesis to searchable text
- embedHypothesis: Compute embedding vectors with caching
- domainSimilarity: Jaccard similarity for domain overlap
- computeSimilarity: Weighted multi-field similarity scoring
- findSimilarHypotheses: Find similar hypotheses with filtering
- searchHypothesesByText: Free-text search across hypotheses
- clusterSimilarHypotheses: Group similar hypotheses
- findDuplicates: Detect potential duplicate hypotheses
- getSimilarityStats: Collection statistics

All 54 tests pass.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch: @@ -417,7 +417,7 @@ {"id":"brenner_bot-augk.1","title":"Task: Decide binding persistence + Agent Mail project strategy","description":"# Goal Make an explicit design decision for how `SessionBinding` is persisted and how it interacts with Agent Mail's `project_key` model. --- # Core question A research session lives (conversation + artifacts) in the `brenner_bot` repo, but experiments run in a target repo. Agent Mail projects are keyed by `human_key` (absolute directory). We need to decide: 1) Where do the *messages* live? (almost certainly: the BrennerBot project) 2) Where do file reservations live? (BrennerBot only vs target repo too) 3) Where does the binding metadata live? (kickoff body vs compiled artifact front matter vs a dedicated binding file) --- # Recommended stance (draft) - Messages + thread live in **BrennerBot Agent Mail project** (single source of truth). - Target repo binding is referenced in: - kickoff message body (human-visible) - compiled artifact YAML front matter (durable) - File reservations: - optional v0: reserve in target repo by calling Agent Mail `file_reservation_paths` in the target repo's own project (requires registering the same agent name in that project) - do NOT split the conversation across projects --- # Why this is worth deciding early If we get this wrong, we break the join-key contract or make cross-repo sessions impossible to reproduce. --- # Acceptance criteria - [ ] Written decision: where binding metadata is persisted - [ ] Written decision: whether/how we do cross-repo file reservations - [ ] Written decision: what is considered v0 vs v1 scope ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:01:27.286071844-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T19:33:09.461762446-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T19:33:09.461762446-05:00","close_reason":"Superseded by v0 decision: use Agent Mail project_key as the research workspace (start sessions in the repo/folder being studied); avoid cross-repo target binding in v0.","labels":["agent-mail","binding","experiments"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-augk.1","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-augk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:01:27.315043637-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-augk.2","title":"Task: Add session start flags + store binding in artifact metadata","description":"# Goal Implement the first usable form of session→target binding. --- # CLI requirements - `./brenner.ts session start` accepts: - `--target-repo ` (required for experiment mode) - optional: `--target-branch`, `--target-remote`, `--results-path`, `--experiment-command` - The kickoff message body includes a clear "Target repo" block. --- # Artifact requirements - Compiled artifacts should include binding metadata in YAML front matter, so later readers know: - what repo was being studied - which revision/branch (if provided) --- # Acceptance criteria - [ ] `session start` supports `--target-repo` - [ ] `session compile/write/publish` preserve binding metadata into YAML front matter - [ ] Unit tests cover flag parsing and metadata persistence ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:01:41.992505748-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T19:33:09.487337884-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T19:33:09.487337884-05:00","close_reason":"Superseded by v0 decision: use Agent Mail project_key as the research workspace (start sessions in the repo/folder being studied); avoid cross-repo target binding in v0.","labels":["binding","cli","experiments"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-augk.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-augk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:01:42.020732228-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-augk.3","title":"Task: Cross-repo file reservations (optional, advisory)","description":"# Goal Allow the operator/orchestrator to reserve files in the target repo via Agent Mail, without moving the main thread out of BrennerBot's project. --- # Background File reservations prevent agent clobbering, but Agent Mail reservations are scoped to a `project_key`. For cross-repo sessions we likely need to reserve paths in: - BrennerBot repo (artifacts, specs) - Target repo (code under study) --- # Proposed approach - Ensure the target repo is also an Agent Mail "project" (ensure_project). - Register the same agent identity name in the target project. - Call `file_reservation_paths(project_key=, paths=[...])`. --- # Risks / considerations - This creates a second Agent Mail mailbox archive (in the target repo), but we will NOT use it for conversation. - Needs careful UX so operators understand reservations are advisory. --- # Acceptance criteria - [ ] CLI can reserve a path glob in the target repo project - [ ] Failure is fail-soft (warn, do not break session) unless explicitly required ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:01:55.767749794-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T19:33:09.488664772-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T19:33:09.488664772-05:00","close_reason":"Superseded by v0 decision: use Agent Mail project_key as the research workspace (start sessions in the repo/folder being studied); avoid cross-repo target binding in v0.","labels":["agent-mail","experiments","file-reservations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-augk.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-augk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:01:55.789236605-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} -{"id":"brenner_bot-av1w","title":"Improve crypto and ID generation robustness","description":"Improve cryptographic operations and ID generation: - prediction-lock.ts: Use globalThis.crypto.subtle with proper error throwing (no weak fallback) - prediction-lock.ts: Use crypto.randomUUID() for prediction lock IDs - calibration.ts: Improve prediction ID generation format - session-kickoff.ts, session-context.tsx, session-replay.ts: Related updates All tests pass (4292 tests).","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2026-01-05T22:03:51.98759224-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T22:03:59.609377279-05:00","labels":["infrastructure","web"]} +{"id":"brenner_bot-av1w","title":"Improve crypto and ID generation robustness","description":"Improve cryptographic operations and ID generation: - prediction-lock.ts: Use globalThis.crypto.subtle with proper error throwing (no weak fallback) - prediction-lock.ts: Use crypto.randomUUID() for prediction lock IDs - calibration.ts: Improve prediction ID generation format - session-kickoff.ts, session-context.tsx, session-replay.ts: Related updates All tests pass (4292 tests).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2026-01-05T22:03:51.98759224-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T22:04:29.967176053-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-05T22:04:29.967176053-05:00","close_reason":"Infrastructure improvements committed: improved crypto handling and ID generation","labels":["infrastructure","web"]} {"id":"brenner_bot-b04","title":"Task: Configure test coverage reporting","description":"Set up test coverage collection and reporting. ## Requirements 1. Coverage thresholds (warn at 60%, fail at 40%) 2. Coverage report generation (lcov, html) 3. Coverage diff on PRs 4. Badge generation for README ## Tools - @vitest/coverage-v8 for collection - GitHub Actions for reporting - Optional: Codecov or similar service ## Output - Coverage report in CI artifacts - Coverage summary in PR comments - Coverage badge for README","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:12:17.89716331-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T23:42:52.535792674-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T23:42:52.535792674-05:00","close_reason":"Coverage already configured in vitest.config.ts with v8 provider, lcov/html reporters, and 50% thresholds","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-b04","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-djz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:12:31.086219282-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-b04","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-0fs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:12:31.215251062-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-b1uy","title":"TASK: Offline Mode & Network Resilience","description":"Handle network failures gracefully. Include: Service worker for offline access, queued actions when offline, reconnection detection and sync, clear UI indicators for offline state, graceful degradation when Agent Mail is unavailable. Users may work on planes or with spotty connections.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:35:39.275156024-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T09:31:10.376049195-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-05T09:31:10.376049195-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented offline resilience: service worker + offline fallback page, queued session kickoffs/actions when offline, network status badge + auto-flush on reconnect.","labels":["brenner-loop","infrastructure"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-b1uy","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1v26.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:36:14.501340496-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-b2o","title":"TanStack Form: Upgrade Session Creation Form","description":"# Goal Upgrade the session creation form to use TanStack Form with Zod validation for better UX and maintainability. ## Background The session creation form (/sessions/new) has 4 fields: - Thread ID (required, unique identifier format) - Hypothesis (required, text) - Prior Beliefs (required, text) - Target Agent (required, selection) Current implementation uses native HTML form with server action: ```typescript
